checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the text content found in the source PDF. There are no fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All headings and body text entries correspond to the agenda items and meeting details present in the source document. The AI-generated alt text and table summaries are permitted additions.
The meeting address '3514 Bush Street, Raleigh, NC' is correctly transcribed from the source. No other contact information (phone, email, URLs) is present in the source or the output.
Both pages of the source PDF are accounted for in the output. All 16 agenda items, the committee meeting list, the next board meeting notice, and the logistics footer are present.

The source table contains a header row plus 19 data rows (including introductory items like 'Call to Order' and 'Adjourn'). The JSON output contains exactly one table with 20 rows total (1 header + 19 data), matching the source count exactly.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All detailed financial figures in agenda item 5 (NG 911, CMRS, PSAP, and Grant funds) are preserved correctly with no omissions. Sub-items for agenda items 1, 6, and 11 are also fully present. All presenter names and time allocations are included.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source precisely. There are no changes to wording that alter the meaning of the agenda items, dates, times, or financial figures.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the JSON output follows the source document page by page without any repeated blocks or sections. Each agenda item and committee meeting entry appears exactly once in the output, mirroring the original document.

The heading hierarchy is correctly represented with an H2 for the main meeting title followed by H3 levels for the sub-sections on committee and board meetings. This maintains a logical, non-skipping structure. The main 'AGENDA' title is captured as a section title.

The two-page agenda table is correctly reconstructed as a single semantic table. The columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter) are maintained accurately, and the row structure correctly groups the item numbers with their respective topics and presenters. Multi-line content within cells (like the financial figures in Item 5) is preserved correctly.

The JSON follows the natural top-to-bottom reading order of the source PDF. It moves from the header information into the main agenda table and then to the supplemental meeting information at the end of the second page.

The bulleted list under 'AUGUST 2019 COMMITTEE MEETINGS' is correctly identified as a list element. The alphabetical and numerical sub-items within agenda topics are correctly kept as text within their respective table cells, preserving their meaning and context.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The JSON contains 3 headings, and the HTML contains 5. The difference of 2 is within the acceptable threshold (the larger of 10% of the JSON count or 3).
There are no links in the source or the JSON. The links reported in the HTML metadata (4) are standard additions by the pipeline (e.g., skip links, ToC links) and do not constitute a defect.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully replicates the multi-column table layout of the agenda items from the source PDF. The high-level organization—header, main agenda table, and committee meeting lists—is preserved in the correct logical order. Information is found in the same relative locations as the source.

The main agenda is rendered as a valid HTML table with clear column headers (Tab, Topic, Presenter) and bordered cells. Headers are visually distinct using background styling. While complex content within cells loses some formatting (evaluated in V5), the table structure itself is sound and readable.
The NC DIT 911 Board logos from page 1 and page 2 of the source PDF are both present in the HTML output as Base64 encoded images with appropriate alt text. No meaningful images are missing.
There are no instances of content duplication. The content flows linearly from the end of page 1 to the beginning of page 2 without repeating headers or paragraphs.

The visual quality is degraded within several table cells (specifically items 5 and 6) because multi-line content and sub-items from the PDF have been collapsed into single text blocks. In the source PDF, financial figures and sub-points are clearly separated on new lines with indentation/alignment. In the HTML, they run together without proper spacing, making specific sections like the 'NG 911 Fund' summary difficult to parse.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Item 5: 
b) NG 911 Fund 
   June 2019 Fund Balance $ 44,749,222
   June 2019 Disbursement -$ 21,114
